name: Lactose Digestion Ability
description: This trait estimates whether your body can digest lactose (milk sugar) as an adult.
simple_summary: You are more likely to either digest lactose comfortably or feel digestive discomfort from milk sugar.
technical_summary: This educational trait uses rs4988235 and rs182549 as proxy markers linked to lactase persistence patterns. It is intended for learning and exploration, not diagnosis.

sources.name: NCBI dbSNP
sources.notes: Variant reference page for the main lactase-persistence marker.

sources.name: NCBI dbSNP
sources.notes: Second marker used in the curated educational rule set.

sources.name: NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog
sources.notes: Curated summary source for lactase-persistence association literature.

rules.description: Regulatory variant near the LCT gene associated with continued lactase enzyme production in adulthood.

genotype_meanings.meaning: This marker suggests reduced lactase persistence in adulthood.
genotype_meanings.effect: Lower likelihood of adult lactase persistence

genotype_meanings.meaning: This marker suggests lactase persistence is more likely in adulthood.
genotype_meanings.effect: Higher likelihood of adult lactase persistence

rules.description: Variant in the MCM6 region that influences expression of the nearby LCT gene involved in lactose digestion.

genotype_meanings.meaning: This marker suggests reduced lactase persistence in adulthood.
genotype_meanings.effect: Lower likelihood of adult lactase persistence

genotype_meanings.meaning: This marker suggests lactase persistence is more likely in adulthood.
genotype_meanings.effect: Higher likelihood of adult lactase persistence

name: Caffeine Processing Speed
description: This trait estimates how quickly your body is likely to process caffeine.
simple_summary: You are more likely to clear caffeine faster or feel it for longer.
technical_summary: This educational trait uses rs762551 in CYP1A2 as a proxy marker for caffeine metabolism speed. It reflects probability, not a guaranteed outcome.

sources.name: NCBI dbSNP
sources.notes: Variant reference page for the CYP1A2 marker used here.

sources.name: NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog
sources.notes: Association summary for caffeine metabolism literature.

rules.description: Variant in the CYP1A2 gene affecting the rate at which caffeine is metabolized in the liver.

genotype_meanings.meaning: This marker suggests faster caffeine metabolism.
This marker suggests slower caffeine metabolism.

name: Alcohol Flush Reaction
description: This trait estimates your likelihood of facial flushing after drinking alcohol.
simple_summary: You are more likely to experience stronger or weaker flushing after alcohol.
technical_summary: This educational trait uses rs671 in ALDH2 as a proxy marker for alcohol flush response. It is for interpretation and learning, not medical advice.

sources.name: NCBI dbSNP
sources.notes: Variant reference page for the ALDH2 marker.

rules.description: Variant in the ALDH2 gene affecting the breakdown of acetaldehyde, a byproduct of alcohol metabolism.

genotype_meanings.meaning: This marker suggests higher likelihood of alcohol flushing.
This marker suggests lower likelihood of alcohol flushing.

name: Likely Eye Color Pattern
description: This trait estimates whether your genetics lean toward lighter or darker eye color.
simple_summary: You are more likely to have lighter eyes or darker eyes in this simplified model.
technical_summary: This educational trait uses rs12913832 in the HERC2/OCA2 region as a compact proxy signal. Eye color is polygenic, so this remains a simplified approximation.

sources.name: NCBI dbSNP
sources.notes: Variant reference page for the HERC2/OCA2 marker.

rules.description: Regulatory variant in the HERC2/OCA2 region influencing melanin production and eye pigmentation.

genotype_meanings.meaning: This marker suggests a higher likelihood of lighter eye color.
This marker suggests a higher likelihood of darker eye color.

name: Bitter Taste Response
description: This trait estimates how strongly you may perceive bitter tastes.
simple_summary: You are more likely to notice bitter flavors strongly or find them milder.
technical_summary: This educational trait uses rs713598 in TAS2R38 as a proxy marker for bitter taste perception. It is a simplified signal for learning and exploration.

sources.name: NCBI dbSNP
sources.notes: Variant reference page for the TAS2R38 marker.

sources.name: NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog
sources.notes: Association summary for bitter taste literature.

rules.description: Variant in the TAS2R38 gene affecting sensitivity to certain bitter-tasting compounds.

genotype_meanings.meaning: This marker suggests higher bitter taste sensitivity.
This marker suggests lower bitter taste sensitivity.
